body:
TUNGSTEN 9.40 -- COMPILED CLEAN (0 errors, 0 warnings), sent to files/from-agent/. Scope actually completed this pass, vs the 5-item priority list from the thesis: items 1-2 (the committed core) fully done, item 5 done, items 3-4 deliberately NOT attempted (honest miss, not silently skipped -- explained below).

WHAT CHANGED, AND WHY (traced to specific thesis arguments):

1. GHOST R-MULTIPLE REALISM (RecordGhostTrade, thesis item 1, highest priority): every TP hit was scored as a flat 2.5R, every SL hit a flat -1.5R, every timeout a score-gated flat 1.5R/-0.8R guess -- regardless of the trade's actual TP/SL distance, which DetermineOptimalTPSL sets dynamically per regime/session/volatility. Phase 6 (the go-live gate) computes profit factor from these numbers, so the gate was validating a simplified fiction of the system's real behavior -- same root cause as the fabricated live R-multiples fixed in 9.39, just in the calibration path. Now: TP hit = actual reward distance / actual risk distance; SL hit = -1.0R (a full stop is -1R by definition, the old -1.5 was actually wrong on its own terms); timeout = actual close price at the timeout bar vs entry, same risk-distance denominator, no more flat guess.

2. UNIFIED GROWTH-RATE FORMULA (thesis item 2): RunMarketCalibration()'s "GAME PLAN" projection and E99_CalculatePathToMillion()'s live projection were two different formulas answering "how fast will this compound" -- one additive (edge-per-trade x trades/day x risk%), one half-Kelly geometric using live rolling WR/RR. Extracted the half-Kelly geometric math (the more rigorous of the two, per your thesis) into CalculateDefensibleDailyGrowthRateCore(p, R, tradesPerDay, ...), parameterized so both call sites share the same formula -- GAME PLAN now calls it with ghost/OOS stats (pre-live), the live path still calls it with live rolling stats, same as before. One formula, two legitimate input sources, instead of two formulas.

5. DetectCurrentRegime() CORRELATION-OVERRIDE AMBIGUITY (thesis item 5): resolved as UNFINISHED WIRING, not intentional. Reasoning: the correlation override (_finalReg = corrRegime when Phase 2 correlation accuracy >= 65%) only ever lived in a local variable returned to whichever caller invoked DetectCurrentRegime() directly. Several OTHER call sites (position sizing, TP/SL RR selection at g_RegimeState.currentRegime==0||1 check, tier-exit logic) read g_RegimeState.currentRegime directly instead -- so those decisions could silently disagree with the correlation-adjusted regime other code was using, same tick. Also telling: the FIX55 DNA-match code right below this explicitly comments that it "does NOT change the classification" -- the override branch has no equivalent disclaimer, which reads as an omission rather than a deliberate boundary. Fix: when the correlation model is confident AND the current bar independently passes RegimeHasStructuralConfirmation (the same real-time evidence check the raw hysteresis path uses), it now commits to g_RegimeState.currentRegime for real -- updates the transition matrix, regime-change bar, logs a REGIME line -- instead of silently diverging. Deliberately did NOT just overwrite state unconditionally: gated on structural confirmation so a historically-confident correlation still needs current-bar corroboration before it can move live regime state, preserving the hysteresis system's anti-whipsaw intent rather than bypassing it.

THIS ONE IS A JUDGMENT CALL, NOT A CERTAINTY -- flagging for your review specifically since it changes live regime-detection behavior (items 1-2 are pure measurement-accuracy fixes with no behavior-changing judgment call; this one has one). If you disagree with the "unfinished wiring" read, the change is localized to one block in DetectCurrentRegime() and easy to revert or re-gate differently.

NOT DONE (items 3-4, explicitly not attempted rather than half-done): higher-timeframe regime as a 7th Phase-4 ProbDB dimension, and routing the currency-strength/cross-pair signal through Phase 2's IC process. Both are real structural additions (new data dimensions, new tracking arrays) that the thesis itself flagged as lower-priority/stretch, and Section 3's whole diagnosis is that this system's failure mode is exactly "changes made without fully checking what else depends on them" -- attempting two new structural additions in the same pass as items 1/2/5, on top of an already-large diff, risked reproducing that same failure mode rather than avoiding it. Saying so plainly rather than shipping something half-wired and calling it done.

BEFORE VS 9.39: 9.39 was a coherence pass (fixed real bugs: dead SL-adjustment code, fabricated live R-multiples, Bayesian belief reset, save/load format drift). 9.40 continues that same "does the system's self-report match its actual behavior" thread but in the calibration/ghost-validation path specifically -- the thesis's honest framing (Section 4) is right: this doesn't invent new edge, it makes the system's confidence in its existing edge trustworthy, which is what makes half-Kelly sizing, regime trust, and the Phase 6 go-live gate mean what they claim to mean.

Compiled with MetaEditor CLI, log confirms 0 errors / 0 warnings on this build.
